word: G3467 - μυωπάζω - myōpázō - moo-ope-ad'-zo - Cannot see far off

sections.paragraph:
- Original: μυωπάζω

- Transliteration: Muopazo

- Phonetic: moo-ope-ad'-zo

- Definition:

1. to see dimly, see only what is near

- Origin: from a compound of the base of G3466 and ops (the face, from G3700)

- TDNT entry: None

- Part(s) of speech: Verb

- Strong's: From a compound of the base of G3466 and ωψō ps (the face: from G3700); to shut the eyes that is blink (see indistinctly): - cannot see afar off.
